my_thoughts: The criterion/redefinition works: value = teleological (functional, not hedonic), redefines: 'i' = functional (teleological purpose), prioritizes: teleology (not pleasure). The student questioned value (seeking validation for observer ego), which shows they understand: wrong separation = should have weight (compromise). They'll likely accept the criterion/redefinition. The episode is complete: definition → mechanism definition → validation → criterion/redefinition. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Two-turn pattern: T1: Student requested definition (seeking error mechanism)—mechanism definition: wrong separation = hedonic/pleasure-based, true 'i' = embryo/point. T2: Student questioned value ('Should not some weight be given to it?')—seeking validation for observer ego via questions value and seeks compromise. They understand: wrong separation = should have weight (compromise). The comparison_contrast mechanism sets criterion: value = teleological (functional, not hedonic), redefines: 'i' = functional (teleological purpose), prioritizes: teleology (not pleasure). Pattern complete: definition request → mechanism definition → validation question → criterion/redefinition. They've shifted: value = teleological (not hedonic), 'i' = functional (teleological purpose) (criterion/redefinition).
